concept_description: A 4-player elimination room: once everyone is ready, the north door opens and players who don't move from room1 to room2 are eliminated. Survivors learn how many of them made it.
scenario_sketch:
Three rooms south→north: room1, room2, room3. Players enter room1 via the hub connection at room1's south wall. north_door (plug between room1/room2) starts visible (closed). room2_north_wall (between room2/room3) starts visible. room3 starts hidden.

Timing/triggers:
- timeoutMs: 90000.
- First entry into room1 starts a 5s countdown that fills room1 with filler bots until there are 4 total players.
- onPlayerReady: once there are 4 players AND all are ready, the scenario closes to new joins, hides north_door (doorway opens), and broadcasts the rule "Players that do not continue will be eliminated."
- 2s later, the rule is re-sent as an instruction `rule_move` to every player still in room1.
- 4s after `rule_move`, any player still in room1 is eliminated.
- When a player enters room2, north_door re-closes for them individually (cannot return).
- Once all living players are in room2, wait 1s, then send instruction `fact_{N}` to each survivor (N = survivor count, 1..4), drop room2_north_wall, make room3 visible, and terminate.
- If 0 players survive, no fact is sent; the scenario terminates on timeoutMs.
possible_outcomes_description: Survivor count ranges from 0 to 4, determined by how many of the 4 ready players move from room1 to room2 within 4s of receiving `rule_move`. Each player independently either moves (survives) or stays (eliminated). The fact_{N} instruction is announced only when N≥1; N=0 ends via timeout with no announcement.
outcomes_test_plan: The single behavioral axis is "moves on rule_move" vs "stays". Two personas (mover, stayer) cover both behaviors. The two extreme outcomes — 4 stayers (0 survivors, exercises the timeout/no-fact path) and 4 movers (4 survivors, exercises the fact_4 + room3 reveal path) — together demonstrate every distinct mechanism in the scenario: door open, individual door re-close on entry, elimination of room1 occupants, survivor-count fact announcement, north wall drop, and the no-survivor timeout branch. A mixed outcome would only be a linear combination of these behaviors and is omitted per guidelines.

bot_personas.name: mover
bot_personas.description: Marks ready on join. On receiving the rule_move instruction, walks north through the open door into room2 and stays there.

bot_personas.name: stayer
bot_personas.description: Marks ready on join. Ignores the rule_move instruction and remains in room1 until eliminated.
